description: A clean, full-frame reproduction of Norman Rockwell's famous 1943 oil painting 'Freedom from Want' (one of his Four Freedoms series), used by the U.S. Department of Labor as a Thanksgiving holiday post. The painting depicts a multigenerational white American family gathered around a white-tableclothed dinner table; a smiling grandmother in an apron sets down an enormous roast turkey on a platter while a grandfather looks on, and family members of various ages smile and chat around the table set with water glasses, celery, fruit and covered dishes. The artist's signature 'Norman Rockwell' appears in the lower-left corner. There is no agency-added on-image text, slogan, seal or graphic-design overlay—the image is simply the artwork itself—so it is not an agency-designed meme/graphic; the messaging (gratitude for the 'American Worker,' invocation of nostalgic mid-century Americana) is carried entirely by the accompanying tweet text, which credits the painting. Marked is_designed=false: it is a reproduction of an existing fine-art painting rather than an agency-designed graphic.
notable_text: Norman Rockwell (signature only; no agency-added on-image text)
